title: Maven project lifecycle

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific source-linked draft lesson for Maven project lifecycle; independent technical, source, pedagogy, accessibility, and runnable-example review remains required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: Apache Maven Getting Started Guide; current accessed 2026-07; Creating a Project, Standard Directory Layout, POM, Dependencies, and Build Lifecycle; atomic topic: Maven project lifecycle
sources.claim: A Maven project declares coordinates, conventional source/test layout, dependencies, plugins, and lifecycle phases so a clean environment can reproduce test and package outputs.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A Maven project declares coordinates, conventional source/test layout, dependencies, plugins, and lifecycle phases so a clean environment can reproduce test and package outputs.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: mvn verify compiles main and test sources, runs deterministic tests, and produces a versioned artifact from the committed pom.xml. Your goal is to build, test and package a multi-package application with Maven. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Exceptions, resources and I/O; Lambdas, streams and optionals; Packages and access control; JDK, JVM and Java language. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: A Maven project declares coordinates, conventional source/test layout, dependencies, plugins, and lifecycle phases so a clean environment can reproduce test and package outputs. This model is applied through the declared Java: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: Dependency ranges, unpinned plugins, profiles, repositories, generated sources, local caches, and environment-sensitive tests can still break reproducibility. The learner must reject this documented misconception: If mvn package succeeds once, the pom fully captures the build and the same artifact is guaranteed everywhere.

includes: Declare coordinates, source layout, dependencies, plugins and reproducible test execution.
Build, test and package a multi-package application with Maven.
excludes: Dependency ranges, unpinned plugins, profiles, repositories, generated sources, local caches, and environment-sensitive tests can still break reproducibility.

outcomes: Build, test and package a multi-package application with Maven.

examples.title: Maven project lifecycle: direct language case
examples.situation: mvn verify compiles main and test sources, runs deterministic tests, and produces a versioned artifact from the committed pom.xml.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Java: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: mvn verify compiles main and test sources, runs deterministic tests, and produces a versioned artifact from the committed pom.xml.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Treat the POM and locked build policy as an executable contract, pin relevant plugin and dependency behavior, and verify from a clean environment.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Treat the POM and locked build policy as an executable contract, pin relevant plugin and dependency behavior, and verify from a clean environment.

examples.title: Maven project lifecycle: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: A build succeeds locally only because an undeclared jar exists in the developer cache, so the dependency is declared and CI verifies from a clean cache.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: A build succeeds locally only because an undeclared jar exists in the developer cache, so the dependency is declared and CI verifies from a clean cache.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: Dependency ranges, unpinned plugins, profiles, repositories, generated sources, local caches, and environment-sensitive tests can still break reproducibility.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: If mvn package succeeds once, the pom fully captures the build and the same artifact is guaranteed everywhere.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Build, test and package a multi-package application with Maven.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: If mvn package succeeds once, the pom fully captures the build and the same artifact is guaranteed everywhere.
misconceptions.correction: Treat the POM and locked build policy as an executable contract, pin relevant plugin and dependency behavior, and verify from a clean environment.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: If mvn package succeeds once, the pom fully captures the build and the same artifact is guaranteed everywhere.

analogy.example: a versioned delivery line whose public labels, recipe, inspections, and investigation record make one artifact reproducible can introduce Maven project lifecycle, after the canonical Java: Beginner to Intermediate rule is stated first.
usefulFor: packages, Maven, JUnit, debugging, and project evidence
limitations: access control is not a security boundary and caches, plugins, bytecode, secrets, and platform differences need technical controls
Dependency ranges, unpinned plugins, profiles, repositories, generated sources, local caches, and environment-sensitive tests can still break reproducibility.

recap.summary: You should now be able to build, test and package a multi-package application with Maven. Keep this boundary in view: Dependency ranges, unpinned plugins, profiles, repositories, generated sources, local caches, and environment-sensitive tests can still break reproducibility.
